channel.title: Math on The Mize Collection
channel.description: Recent content in Math on The Mize Collection

item.title: Matrices
item.description:
Matrices are structures that condense multiple numbers into a single entity allowing us to perform operations on them as a whole. Matrices are a fundamental concept in linear algebra and used a lot in computer graphics.

Matrix Operations

Matrix Addition

Matrix Subtraction

Matrix Multiplication

Identity Matrix

Inverse Matrix

Transpose Matrix

Row Order vs Column Order

Depending on the convention, we can have a row vector or a column vector. The row order will organize the matrix in a row-wise manner. This means that the first row is the first vector.

item.title: Vectors
item.description:
Definition

In math, a vector is object that has a magnitude and a direction. The components of a vector are the numbers that describe the vector in a coordinate system. A common coordinate system is the Cartesian coordinate system which uses the x, y, and z axes.

In programming, we can implement a vector as an array of numbers. A 2D vector, for example, can be defined as an array of 2 floats. A 3D vector can be defined as an array of 3 floats.
